2605.10820.title: MaD Physics: Evaluating information seeking under constraints in physical environments
2605.10820.paper_summary: Scientific discovery is fundamentally a resource-constrained process that requires navigating complex trade-offs between the quality and quantity of measurements due to physical and cost constraints. Measurements drive the scientific process by revealing novel phenomena to improve our understanding. Existing benchmarks for evaluating agents for scientific discovery focus on either static knowledge-based reasoning or unconstrained experimental design tasks, and do not capture the ability to make measurements and plan under constraints. To bridge this gap, we propose Measuring and Discovering Physics (MaD Physics), a benchmark to evaluate the ability of agents to make informative measurements and conclusions subject to constraints on the quality and quantity of measurements. The benchmark consists of three environments, each based on a distinct physical law. To mitigate contamination from existing knowledge, MaD Physics includes altered physical laws. In each trial, the agent makes measurements of the system until it exhausts an allotted budget and then the agent has to infer the underlying physical law to make predictions about the state of the system in the future. MaD Physics evaluates two fundamental capabilities of scientific agents: inferring models from data and planning under constraints. We also demonstrate how MaD Physics can be used to evaluate other capabilities such as multimodality and in-context learning. We benchmark agents on MaD Physics using four Gemini models (2.5 Flash Lite, 2.5 Flash, 2.5 Pro, and 3 Flash), identifying shortcomings in their structured exploration and data collection capabilities and highlighting directions to improve their scientific reasoning.
2605.10820.paper_summary_zh: 科學發現本質上是一個資源受限的過程，因為它需要在物理和成本限制下在測量的質量和數量之間進行複雜的權衡。<lb/>測量推動科學過程，通過揭示新現象來改善我們的理解。<lb/>現有的評估科學發現代理人的基準專注於靜態知識基礎推理或不受限制的實驗設計任務，並未捕捉在約束下進行測量和計劃的能力。<lb/>為了填補這一空白，我們提出了測量與發現物理學（MaD Physics），這是一個評估代理人在質量和數量受限的情況下進行有意義的測量和得出結論能力的基準。<lb/>該基準由三個環境組成，每個環境基於一種獨特的物理法則。<lb/>為了減少來自現有知識的污染，MaD Physics 包括改變的物理法則。<lb/>在每次試驗中，代理人對系統進行測量，直到耗盡分配的預算，然後代理人必須推斷潛在的物理法則，以對系統未來的狀態進行預測。<lb/>MaD Physics 評估科學代理的兩項基本能力：從數據中推斷模型和在約束下進行計劃。<lb/>我們還展示了如何使用 MaD Physics 來評估其他能力，例如多模態和上下文學習。<lb/>我們使用四個 Gemini 模型（2.5 Flash Lite、2.5 Flash、2.5 Pro 和 3 Flash）在 MaD Physics 上對代理進行基準測試，識別它們在結構化探索和數據收集能力方面的不足，並突顯改進其科學推理的方向。
2605.10820.author: Moksh Jain et.al.
2605.10820.authors: Moksh Jain, Mehdi Bennani, Johannes Bausch, Yuri Chervonyi, Bogdan Georgiev, Simon Osindero, Nenad Tomašev
